commentary: BII upgrades infrastructure equity to overweight in the 2026 Global Outlook Q2 Update, underpinned by mega forces including AI buildout, energy transition, and defence spending. Listed infrastructure trades near 2-standard-deviation discounts to global equity — among the cheapest thematic exposures available — and offers structural demand resilience. BlackRock has also acted on this view at the multi-asset level: the MyMap range added a new holding in listed infrastructure during the Q1 update (April), framing it as an undervalued real-economy asset well positioned to benefit from the ongoing expansion in real economic activity, complementing MyMap's value tilt.
notes: [L162-176] The source's central counterweight: mega-cap AI winners are 'particularly vulnerable to profit taking,' their FCF is 'draw(n) down toward zero,' some 'issuing debt,' and S&P 500 FCF yield is the lowest in 25 years — the whole thesis is diversifying AWAY from them. The model emitted zero calls capturing this; it only made positive tech calls. A genuine, costly miss (U vs N arguable, but the caution is unambiguous).
